evidence.note: R1 should be exactly 28 bp (16 bp cell barcode + 12 bp UMI)

findings.title: Cell barcodes on the 10x whitelist

evidence.note: R1[0:16] should match the 3M-february-2018 gel-bead barcode whitelist

findings.detail: 94.9% of cell barcodes are on the whitelist

findings.title: R2 reads beginning with the TSO (adapter-dimer / short insert)

evidence.note: R2 should start with cDNA; a leading TSO is the hallmark of empty/short-insert products

findings.detail: 32.6% of R2 start with the TSO

findings.title: R2 read-through into the Illumina adapter

evidence.note: the AGATCGGAAGAGC adapter stem in R2 means the insert was shorter than the read length

findings.detail: 5.5% of R2 contain the adapter stem AGATCGGAAGAGC

findings.title: R2 with a poly-G no-signal tail

evidence.note: a poly-G 3' tail on two-color instruments is 'no signal' — typical of empty/short fragments

findings.detail: 9.8% of R2 have a poly-G tail

ranked.why: 32.6% of R2 start with the TSO
9.8% of R2 have a poly-G tail
5.5% of R2 contain the adapter stem AGATCGGAAGAGC
94.9% of cell barcodes are on the whitelist

plan.root_cause: R2 reads beginning with the TSO (adapter-dimer / short insert)
plan.diagnosis: r2 reads beginning with the tso (adapter-dimer / short insert) (32.6% of R2 start with the TSO); r2 with a poly-g no-signal tail (9.8% of R2 have a poly-G tail).
